task: Fix race in auditDiscoveryUnlocks that produced misleading 'module not yet present' log on first boot

before_pattern.snippet: const modPath = '../data/discoveryUnlocks.js'; const mod = await import(/* @vite-ignore */ modPath); DISCOVERY_UNLOCKS = mod.default || mod.DISCOVERY_UNLOCKS;
before_pattern.catch_branch_logged: [runtimeAudit] DISCOVERY_UNLOCKS module not yet present — auditDiscoveryUnlocks skipped
before_pattern.race_condition: Dynamic import resolved at runtime racing against module-graph evaluation; on first boot the catch branch fired even though the file existed and was importable, masking real audit findings.

after_pattern.function: auditDiscoveryUnlocks is now sync; reads DISCOVERY_UNLOCKS_REAL directly. Try/catch wraps iteration logic only, NOT the import. Malformed-data path emits a warning and skips iteration without printing the misleading 'module not yet present' message.

build.warnings: Pre-existing chunk-size warnings (vendor-phaser, vendor-rapier, GamePage, vendor-three) unrelated to this change.

smoke_test.method: node ESM dynamic import of src/renderer/game/data/discoveryUnlocks.js (Node-side sanity check; production import is now static)
smoke_test.result: default export resolved to object with 4 keys; first entry desert_foraging => {questId: 'desert_healer', pending: false}
smoke_test.implication: The static import in runtimeAudit.js will resolve at module-graph evaluation time and DISCOVERY_UNLOCKS_REAL is the real object. Audit will now run iteration logic instead of skipping.

race_fixed_note: Original 'DISCOVERY_UNLOCKS module not yet present' log message is gone. The new code-path: (1) static import is resolved by Vite/Rollup at module-graph eval time, no runtime resolution; (2) when runRuntimeAudit() runs, DISCOVERY_UNLOCKS_REAL is already bound; (3) iteration runs in a try/catch, so genuine data corruption emits a warning to data/discoveryUnlocks.js, NOT a misleading 'not present' message; (4) no path remains where the catch can fire on first-boot timing.

constraints_check.graceful_malformed_handling: warning emitted for non-object DISCOVERY_UNLOCKS; iteration try/catch wraps loop; no try/catch around the import itself

scope_note: There is a pre-existing semantic gap (documented inside discoveryUnlocks.js): the audit checks keys against regions.js IDs, but DISCOVERY_UNLOCKS keys are worldMapData location IDs. With this fix the audit will now actually report those mismatches as errors — exactly the kind of structural finding the orchestrator wanted unmasked. Resolving that gap is OUT OF SCOPE for this fix (file edit boundary forbids touching discoveryUnlocks.js, and changing the audit semantics is a separate concern from the timing fix). Flagging here so the next agent considering audit semantics has context.
memory_note: Bug brief instructed: this fix supersedes feedback_dynamic_import_static_resolution.md's mitigation for THIS specific call site only (because discoveryUnlocks.js now exists). The general rule — use dynamic import for genuinely-pending modules — still applies elsewhere. No memory file edits performed.
